claims.claim: The Circuit Tracks playing grid is a 4 x 8 matrix of exactly 32 velocity-sensitive pads, each RGB-illuminated with independent red, green and blue LEDs.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.15 Top View item 1; p.5 Introduction

claims.claim: There are 8 macro rotary encoders (Macro 1-8), each with an RGB LED, whose function changes per View; their movements can be recorded and replayed into a pattern.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.15 Top View item 3

claims.claim: The Master Filter is a large rotary knob with a centre detent and RGB LED that is always active; turning anticlockwise from centre gives a low-pass filter, clockwise gives a high-pass filter, and the LED is dim white at the detent and pale blue when filtering.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.15 item 2; p.94 The Filter Knob

claims.claim: There are 8 track buttons labelled Synth 1, Synth 2, MIDI 1, MIDI 2, Drum 1, Drum 2, Drum 3, Drum 4; pressing any track button always goes to Note View for that track.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.16 item 5; p.14 Glossary 'Track'

claims.claim: The four step buttons are Note, Velocity, Gate and Pattern Settings; Probability is the Shift function of the Pattern Settings button.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.16 items 6-7; p.13 Glossary 'Probability View'

claims.claim: Shift + Note opens Expanded View, which doubles the synth performance-pad area and lets you create patterns on all four drum tracks simultaneously.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.11 Glossary 'Expanded View'

claims.claim: Shift + Velocity toggles Fixed velocity (disabling pad velocity response), and Shift + Gate enters Micro Step editing where each step is subdivided into six micro steps.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.11 Glossary 'Fixed' and 'Micro step'

claims.claim: Shift + Duplicate = Mutate (randomises steps), Shift + FX = Side Chain, Shift + Patterns = View Lock, Shift + Projects = Packs, and Shift + Save = Setup View.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.12-14 Glossary; p.102 Setup View

claims.claim: Setup View is entered by holding Shift and pressing Save, and is exited by pressing Play.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.102 Setup View

claims.claim: Advanced Setup View is entered by holding Shift while powering the unit on and exited by pressing Play; in it the Note button toggles Easy Start/Mass Storage (green on / dim red off), Duplicate toggles MIDI Thru behaviour, and FX toggles the Master Compressor.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.106 Advanced Setup View

claims.claim: Save Lock is toggled by holding both Shift and Save while powering the unit on; while active the Save button stays unlit and the setting persists across power cycles.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.107 Save Lock

claims.claim: Holding Shift + Clear while powering on forces Circuit Tracks to load a blank Project (a corruption recovery gesture).
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.107 Project loading problems

claims.claim: Bootloader Mode is entered by powering off, holding the Scales, Preset and Note buttons, then powering on; it is exited by pressing Play.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.108 Bootloader Mode

claims.claim: The rear panel carries full-size 5-pin DIN MIDI In, Out and Thru connectors (not TRS minijacks), and the Thru can be reconfigured in Advanced Setup View to act as a second MIDI Out.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.18 Rear View item 4; p.106 MIDI Thru configuration

claims.claim: Main audio outputs are two 1/4-inch TS jacks (L/Mono and R) with a maximum output level of +5.3 dBu; with nothing plugged into R, L/Mono carries a mono sum of L and R.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.18 Rear View item 1

claims.claim: There are two external audio inputs (Inputs 1 and 2) on unbalanced 1/4-inch TS jacks at line level; they mix with internal sound, can be processed by the FX section and ducked by the drum tracks.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.18 Rear View item 5; p.88 Mixer

claims.claim: The Sync output is a 3.5 mm TRS jack delivering a 5 V analogue clock; the rate is set in Setup View to 1, 2, 4, 8 or 24 ppqn with a default of 2 ppqn, and swing is not applied to it.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.18 item 2; p.105 Analogue clock rates; p.87 Analogue Sync Out

claims.claim: The headphone output is a 3.5 mm stereo socket; the main outputs stay active when headphones are inserted, and the amp drives +5 dBu into 150 ohm headphones.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.18 Rear View item 3

claims.claim: The USB-C port provides power, charging and class-compliant USB-MIDI, but does NOT carry audio.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.18 Rear View item 6

claims.claim: The internal Lithium-ion battery is non-removable and non-replaceable, gives up to 4 hours of operation, recharges in up to 4 hours, and triggers a red low-battery grid symbol when capacity drops below 15%.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.9-10 Power Requirements / Using the internal battery

claims.claim: The power switch is a soft on/off button requiring an approximately one-second press, with an integral LED that lights green while the internal battery is charging.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.18 Rear View item 8; p.10

claims.claim: Factory default MIDI channels are Synth 1 = channel 1, Synth 2 = channel 2, MIDI 1 = channel 3, MIDI 2 = channel 4, and all four Drum tracks = channel 10; any track can be set to channels 1-15, with channel 16 reserved for the Project and no two tracks allowed on the same channel.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.103 MIDI Channels

claims.claim: In Setup View, pads 25-32 form four coloured Rx/Tx toggle pairs: Note (green, pads 25/26), CC (orange, 27/28), Program Change (purple, 29/30) and Clock (pale blue, 31/32); all default to ON.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.104 MIDI I/O

claims.claim: In Setup View, Pad 24 (lit white) toggles pad brightness, dimming the grid by approximately 50%, and the setting is retained through power-down.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.103 Brightness

claims.claim: The internal tempo runs 40-240 BPM in integer steps with a default of 120 BPM, while when slaved to external MIDI clock it follows 30-300 BPM including fractional values; Tap Tempo (Shift + Tempo/Swing) needs at least three taps and averages the last five.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.85-86 Tempo, External clock, Tap Tempo

claims.claim: Swing has a range of 20 to 80 with a default of 50; values below 50 advance the even (off-beat) steps and values above 50 delay them, adjusted with Macro 2 in Tempo View.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.86-87 Swing

claims.claim: The Click/metronome is toggled by Shift + Clear (Clear lights green when on, dim red when off) and its volume is set with Macro 5 in Tempo View.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.87 Click track

claims.claim: The Play button lights bright green during playback and the Record button lights bright red in Record mode; the playback cursor is a white pad that turns red in Record mode.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.12 Glossary Playback/Record; p.16 item 13

claims.claim: The up/down arrow buttons in Note View shift a track's pitch by octave, up to 5 octaves up and 6 octaves down, independently per track.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.17 item 15

claims.claim: Each synth track can load any of 128 patches (4 pages of 32), each drum track any of 64 samples (2 pages of 32), and each MIDI track any of 8 templates, all via the Preset button.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.16 item 14; p.12-14 Glossary Patch/Sample/Template

claims.claim: The Scales button opens Scales View offering 16 musical scales plus transposition of the synth keyboard.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.13 Glossary 'Scale View'; p.16 item 9

claims.claim: The FX section provides 16 delay presets and 8 reverb presets with per-track send levels on the macros, plus a Master Compressor applied to the whole mix that is toggled by the FX button in Advanced Setup View.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.90-92 FX Section; p.106 Master Compressor

claims.claim: Side Chain View (Shift + FX) offers 7 side-chain presets per synth/MIDI-input track, letting a chosen drum track duck the synths or external inputs; side-chain is off by default.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.93 Side Chains

claims.claim: Mixer View is an 8-channel mixer whose Row 1 pads are per-track mutes; by default the macros control track level (default 100, range 0-127) and pressing the down-arrow switches the macros to pan (white centre, blue left, pink right).
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.88-89 Mixer

claims.claim: Sticky Shift is enabled in Setup View by pressing Shift; once on (Shift lit bright green) a press of Shift latches until pressed again, avoiding held combos.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.17 item 20

claims.claim: The Step Page button (1-16 / 17-32) sets whether a track's pattern is 16 or 32 steps, and its legend colour changes while running to indicate which half of a 32-step sequence is displayed.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.16 item 8

claims.claim: Each track stores 8 patterns per Project, up to 64 Projects are held in internal memory, and up to 32 Packs can be exported to a microSD card.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.12-13 Glossary Pattern memory/Project/Pack; p.5 Introduction

claims.claim: On boot the grid shows a ~5 second boot pattern, then pads change from pale red to bright green sequentially from top-left to bottom-right to indicate Pack loading.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.19 Powering the unit on

claims.claim: The supplied AC adaptor is 5 V DC, 2 A with interchangeable slide-in plug heads and a Type-A USB output; the box also includes a 1.5 m USB-A to USB-C cable.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.7 What's In The Box; p.9 Using the AC adaptor

claims.claim: The rear panel includes a Kensington MiniSaver security slot.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.18 Rear View item 9

claims.claim: Scenes are one of 16 memories to which patterns/pattern-chains are assigned; the manual's Scene glossary entry indicates they are accessed by holding Shift while Mixer View is selected, and the Mixer View lower rows show the Scene pads.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.13 Glossary 'Scene'; p.88 Mixer diagram

claims.claim: Save requires two presses to complete (first press flashes Save, second stores to the last-used Project slot); pressing any other button after the first press aborts the save.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.96 Saving Projects to new slots

claims.claim: When saving, a Project pad colour can be chosen from a palette of 14 colours by rotating Macro 1 after the first Save press.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide V3, p.96 Changing Project Colours

verdicts.note: Top View item 1: '32-pad playing grid – a 4 x 8 matrix of pads; internally illuminated with RGB LEDs.' Introduction: '32 illuminated, velocity-sensitive pads' and footnote '* RGB LED illumination means that each pad has internal red, blue and green LEDs, each of which can light at different [intensities].' Every element (4x8, 32, velocity-sensitive, independent R/G/B LEDs) is confirmed. Circuit Tracks-specific.

Top View item 3: 'Macro controls 1 to 8 – eight multi-functional rotary encoders, with associated RGB LED... The movement of Macro controls in performance may be recorded and replayed.' Function varies per View. Confirmed.

Top View item 2 + p.94 'The Filter Knob': 'centre detent and RGB LED... always active'; 'controls a low-pass filter when you turn it anticlockwise from the centre... and a high-pass filter when you turn it clockwise'; at detent 'no filtering takes place and the LED below the knob is dimly lit white... The LED changes to pale blue when either filter type is active.' All confirmed.
Top View item 5: eight track buttons 'Synth 1 and 2/MIDI 1 and 2/Drum 1 to 4.' Glossary 'Track': 'When you press a Track button, you always go to Note View for that track.' Confirmed.

Top View item 6: 'Step buttons: Note, Velocity, Gate and Probability... Note that Probability is the Shift function of the Pattern Settings button.' The four physical buttons in the row are Note, Velocity, Gate, Pattern Settings, with Probability as the Shift legend on Pattern Settings — confirmed. Terminology nuance: the Glossary's 'Step buttons' entry names the group by function as 'Note, Velocity, Gate and Probability' (not 'Pattern Settings'), but both refer to the same buttons and the load-bearing fact (Probability = Shift of Pattern Settings) is explicit.

Glossary 'Expanded View' (Shift + Note): 'Doubles the area of performance pads for the Synths, and allows you create patterns on all drum tracks simultaneously, using the currently selected samples.' Confirmed.

Glossary: 'Fixed' (Shift + Velocity) 'Allows the velocity response of the grid pads to be disabled'; 'Micro step' (Shift + Gate) 'The interval between consecutive Steps is further subdivided into six micro steps.' Both confirmed.

Glossary: Mutate = Shift + Duplicate ('Randomises the Steps'); Side Chain = Shift + FX; View Lock = Shift + Patterns; Setup Page = Shift + Save. p.97: 'Packs View is the secondary view of the Projects button. Open by holding Shift and pressing [Projects].' All five mappings confirmed.
p.102 Setup View: 'It is entered by holding down Shift and pressing Save, and exited by pressing Play.' Confirmed.

p.106 Advanced Setup View: 'entered by holding down Shift while powering the unit on, and exited by pressing Play.' Note toggles Easy Start/Mass Storage ('If Note is lit bright green, it is enabled, if Note is lit dim red, it is disabled'); Duplicate toggles MIDI Thru behaviour ('bright green' = second MIDI Out, 'dim red' = regular Thru); FX toggles Master Compressor ('enabled, the FX button lights bright green: when it is disabled, it lights dim red'). All confirmed.

p.107 Save Lock: 'To enable Save Lock, hold both Shift and Save down while powering the unit on. While Save Lock is enabled, the Save button is unlit at all times. Save Lock status is retained during subsequent power cycles.' Confirmed.

p.107 Project loading problems: 'we have included a method of turning Circuit Tracks on and forcing it to load a blank Project instead. To do this, hold down both Shift and Clear while turning Circuit Tracks on.' Confirmed.

p.108 Bootloader Mode: '1. Turn Circuit Tracks off 2. Hold down the Scales, Preset and Note buttons 3. Power Circuit Tracks on again... Bootloader Mode is most easily exited by simply pressing the Play button.' Confirmed.

Rear View item 4: 'MIDI In, Out and Thru – standard set of three MIDI connectors on 5-pin DIN sockets... the MIDI Thru port may be configured in Advanced Setup View to act as a clone of the MIDI Out port.' Full-size 5-pin DIN (not TRS) confirmed; Thru-as-second-Out confirmed (p.106: 'When Duplicate is lit bright green, the MIDI Thru port will act as a second MIDI Out').

Rear View item 1: 'Outputs - L/Mono and R... on two 1/4" TS jack sockets. Max. output level is +5.3 dBu (+/-1.5 dBu). With no plug in the R socket, the L/Mono socket carries a mono mix of L and R channels.' Confirmed.

Rear View item 5: 'Inputs 1 and 2 – two external audio inputs: line level signals... can be mixed with the internally-generated sounds, and can be individually treated by the FX section. They can also be ducked by the drum tracks. The inputs are unbalanced on 1/4" TS jack sockets.' Confirmed.

Rear View item 2 + p.105 + p.87: 'Sync – a 3.5 mm TRS jack socket supplying a clock signal of 5 V amplitude... The default rate is two pulses per quarter note.' p.105 Analogue clock rates: selectable 1, 2, 4, 8 or 24 ppqn (pads 17-21), default 2 ppqn, and 'Swing (if set to something other than 50%) is not applied to the analogue clock output.' All confirmed.

Rear View item 3: 'The main outputs remain active when a plug is inserted. The headphone amplifier can drive +5 dBu into a pair of 150 ohm stereo headphones.' The PDF rear-view text does not state the jack size, but Novation's official specs / Thomann and Sound on Sound confirm the headphone output is a 3.5 mm stereo mini-jack. Confirmed.

Rear View item 6: 'USB-C port. This is also the DC power input... battery charging... The port is MIDI class compliant... Also used for firmware updates. NOTE – Circuit Tracks' USB port does not carry audio.' Confirmed.

p.10 Using the internal battery: 'The battery is non-removable and non-replaceable. Circuit Tracks will operate for up to 4 hours... When the battery capacity drops below 15%, a Low Battery symbol will be displayed... charging time is up to 4 hours.' All core facts confirmed. Minor: the manual text does not explicitly state the low-battery grid symbol is 'red' (it shows an icon), though that is consistent with the graphic.

Rear View item 8: 'soft on/off switch; to prevent inadvertent power up/down, a press of approx. one second is needed to turn the unit on or off. The button has an integral LED which lights green to indicate that the internal battery is charging.' Confirmed.

p.103 MIDI Channels: factory defaults Synth 1=1, Synth 2=2, MIDI 1=3, MIDI 2=4, Drums 1-4=10. 'Each track... can be set to any of MIDI channels 1-15. Channel 16 is reserved for the Project... all four drum tracks use the same MIDI channel... No two tracks can transmit on the same MIDI channel.' All confirmed.

p.104 MIDI I/O: Pads 25-32 = four pairs — 25/26 MIDI Note (Green), 27/28 MIDI CC (Orange), 29/30 MIDI Program Change (Purple), 31/32 MIDI Clock (Pale Blue), each Rx/Tx. 'By default, both MIDI Rx and MIDI Tx are ON.' Exactly confirmed.

p.103 Brightness: 'Pad 24 (illuminated white) controls the brightness of the grid pads. The default setting is for full brightness, but pressing Pad 24 dims them by approximately 50%... The brightness setting is saved when Circuit Tracks is powered down.' Confirmed.

p.85-86: internal tempo 'range 40 to 240 BPM; the default tempo for a new Project is 120 BPM' and 'the internal tempo clock only allows integer BPMs.' External clock: 'will synchronise to any external clock rates – including fractional values - in the range 30 to 300 BPM.' Tap Tempo: 'Hold down Shift and tap the Tempo/Swing button... You need at least three taps... and it will then calculate the BPM by averaging the last five taps.' All confirmed.

p.86-87 Swing: default 50, 'the range is 20 to 80'; 'a lower swing value shortens the time between an even step and the previous odd step' (advances even off-beats), higher delays them; 'Swing is adjusted with Macro 2, while in Tempo View.' Diagrams show 'Even beats are advanced' at 25%, 'Even beats are delayed' at 75%. Confirmed.

p.87 Click track: 'activated or deactivated by holding Shift and pressing Clear. Clear will light bright green when Click is enabled and dim red when it is not... To adjust the volume of the Click, press Tempo/Swing and use Macro 5.' Confirmed.

Glossary: 'Playback Mode... the Play button will be lit bright green'; 'Record Mode... The Record button will be lit bright red'; 'Playback Cursor... the white pad which moves through the pattern display... Changes to red in Record Mode.' Confirmed.

Top View item 15: in Note View the up/down arrows 'let you shift the pitch of the synth pads or the MIDI tracks up by one to five octaves, or down by one to six octaves when entering notes: the pitch range of each track is adjustable independently.' Confirmed.

Top View item 14: 'Each synth track can use any of 128 Patches, each MIDI track can use any of eight MIDI templates, and each drum track can use any of 64 percussion samples. The synth and drum Presets are arranged in pages of 32.' Glossary confirms Patch (128, four pages of 32), Sample (64, two pages of 32), Template (eight). All via Preset button. Confirmed.

Top View item 9 + Glossary 'Scale View' (Scales): 'allows the selection of one of sixteen different music scales for the synth keyboard, and also lets you transpose the synth keyboard to a higher or lower key.' Confirmed.

p.90-92 FX Section: 'Sixteen delay and eight reverb presets are provided... The send levels from each track... are individually adjustable for each track using the Macro controls. There is also a master compressor which is applied to your mix by default.' Master compressor toggled by FX button in Advanced Setup View (p.106). Confirmed.

p.93 Side Chains: 'Seven Side Chain presets are available, each allowing the selected drum track to modify the sound of the synth tracks (or signals at the external audio inputs)... The default state is for Side Chain to be OFF on both synths and both MIDI tracks.' 'Side Chain View is the secondary view of the FX button... Open by holding Shift and pressing FX.' Confirmed.

p.88-89 Mixer: eight-channel mixer; 'The illuminated pads on Row 1 are Mute buttons for each track.' 'By default... the Macros control volume level of each track' (default 100, range 0-127). Pan colors: 'default pan position... stereo-centre, indicated by the Macro LEDs showing white. Panning a track left turns the LED increasingly bright blue; panning to the right turns it increasingly bright pink.' The official online guide confirms the down arrow (down-arrow/▼ is lit by default for level) switches macros to pan: 'the Macros control volume level... indicated by the ▼ button being lit. Pressing the ▼ button converts the Macro controls to pan controls.' Down-arrow-to-pan confirmed. (Note: the PDF text renders the arrow glyphs ambiguously as 'J'/'K'; the online HTML guide resolves it as ▼ = level default → press ▼ for pan.)

Top View item 20: 'To enable Sticky Shift, enter Setup View and press Shift. Sticky Shift is on when the Shift button is bright green.' 'When you press Shift it acts as if it is held until you press it a second time.' Confirmed.

Top View item 8: 'Step Page (1-16/17-32) – selects whether the Pattern for the currently selected track is initially 16 or 32 steps in length. When a 32-step Pattern is selected, the colour of the button legend changes when the sequence is running to indicate which "half" of the sequence the grid is currently displaying. You can choose to have a 16- or 32-step Pattern on any track.' Confirmed.

Glossary: 'Pattern memory... there are eight per track in each Project'; 'Up to 64 Projects can be saved as a Pack in flash memory'; 'Pack... Up to 32 Packs can be exported to a Micro SD card for external storage.' All confirmed.

p.19 Powering the unit on: 'the grid will show the boot-up display for approximately five seconds... After initial boot-up, the display will change colour from pale red to bright green sequentially from top left to bottom right, indicating Pack loading.' Confirmed.

p.7 What's In The Box: 'USB Type A to Type C cable (1.5 m)' and 'AC adaptor: 5 V DC, 2 A; includes interchangeable AC plugs.' p.9: '5 V DC, 2 A type with a Type A USB output... interchangeable slide-in AC plug heads.' All confirmed.
Rear View item 9: 'Kensington MiniSaver – secure your Circuit Tracks to a suitable structure if desired.' Confirmed.

Glossary 'Scene': 'One of 16 memories to which multiple Patterns and Pattern Chains can be assigned... Hold Shift (while Mixer View is selected).' p.81/88: 'The two lower rows of pads in Mixer View represent the 16 Scenes.' To assign/store: 'Change to Mixer View, press and hold Shift: the Scene pads change colour to dim gold.' Confirmed. (Selecting an existing Scene only needs a pad press in Mixer View; the Shift gesture is for assigning — the claim's framing is accurate.)

p.96 Saving Projects to new slots: 'Save needs to be pressed twice to complete the store process: the first press will flash the Save button; a second press will save your work to the last Project memory that was in use... you can "abort" the Save routine after the first press of Save by pressing any other button.' Confirmed.

p.96 Changing Project Colours: 'After pressing Save for the first time, the LED under Macro 1 rotary control will light in the current colour of the pad... You can now scroll through a palette of 14 colours by rotating the Macro 1 knob.' Confirmed.

verification.overallNotes:
Source used: official Novation Circuit Tracks User Guide, Version 3 (© 2022 Focusrite Audio Engineering Ltd), fetched from the Kraft Music mirror of the Novation PDF and cross-checked against Novation's online user-guide articles (userguides.novationmusic.com) and product specs (Thomann, Sound on Sound). All content is explicitly Circuit Tracks (the 2-synth/2-MIDI/4-drum 2020 device) — NOT the original Circuit or Circuit Rhythm. The guide's own footer page numbers match the page citations in the claims. All 43 hardware claims are CONFIRMED against the official manual.

Two very minor caveats worth surfacing (both still 'confirmed'): (a) hardware-18 — the headphone jack being 3.5 mm is NOT stated in the manual's rear-view text (which only gives the +5 dBu/150 ohm drive spec); it is confirmed instead by Novation's published specs and retailer/press specs. (b) hardware-20 — the manual says a 'Low Battery symbol' appears below 15% but does not literally call it 'red' in the prose (the icon graphic is red). (c) hardware-5 terminology: the manual's Glossary names the step-button group as 'Note, Velocity, Gate and Probability', while the physical fourth button is labelled 'Pattern Settings' with Probability as its Shift legend — the claim's wording ('four step buttons are Note, Velocity, Gate and Pattern Settings') is hardware-accurate but differs from the manual's chosen group name. (d) hardware-34: the PDF's arrow glyphs extract ambiguously as 'J'/'K'; the online HTML guide disambiguates them — the DOWN arrow (▼) is lit by default for Level and pressing it switches Macros to Pan, so the claim is correct.

IMPORTANT HARDWARE FACTS THE CLAIMS MISSED ENTIRELY:
1. Master Volume control (Top View item 4): a dedicated physical rotary that 'controls the overall level of Circuit Tracks' audio outputs' — a top-panel hardware control not covered by any claim. This is the most notable omission.
2. Momentary vs latching button behaviour (Top View, between items 4-5): 'Many buttons have both a momentary (long press) and a latching (short press) mode. A long press temporarily displays that button's view only while held; a short press switches the grid view permanently.' The Record button is a special case whose momentary action allows rapid punch-in/punch-out. This is a fundamental hardware-interaction model none of the claims capture.
3. Three power methods (p.9): powered from (a) a computer USB 3.0 port via USB-C, (b) AC mains adaptor via USB-C, or (c) internal Li-ion battery — the claims cover battery/adaptor/USB-C individually but not the explicit '3 ways to power' framing, notably charging from a computer's USB 3.0 port.
4. The up/down arrow (▼/▲) buttons double as a page-2 selector in several Views (e.g., selecting from 8 Patterns while only 4 are displayed; switching Synth vs MIDI Side Chain pages) — beyond the octave-shift role in hardware-29.
5. microSD card slot as a distinct rear-panel hardware feature (Rear View item 7) — referenced only indirectly via Packs in hardware-37.
6. Track colour-coding scheme (Synth 1 = violet, Synth 2 = pale green, MIDI 1 = blue, MIDI 2 = pink, Drums = orange, etc.) used consistently across all Views for rapid identification — a hardware/UX identity element.
7. The AC adaptor operates on 100-240 V, 50/60 Hz with multiple country plug heads (partially in hardware-39 but the wide-voltage range is worth noting).
8. Physical dimensions/weight are NOT in this user guide (they are on Novation's product/spec pages), so no claim could be checked against the manual for those.

verdicts.note: Confirmed verbatim. Guide p.15 item 1: '32-pad playing grid – a 4 x 8 matrix of pads; internally illuminated with RGB LEDs.' Intro (p.5/spec list): 'RGB grid with 32 velocity-sensitive pads.' Footnote: 'each pad has internal red, blue and green LEDs, each of which can light at different intensities.' Explicitly Circuit Tracks.

Guide p.15 item 3: 'Macro controls 1 to 8 – eight multi-functional rotary encoders, with associated RGB LED. The availability and function of these controls varies with Circuit Track's various views... The movement of Macro controls in performance may be recorded and replayed.' Recording confirmed again p.34/Record Mode section.

Fully confirmed. p.15 item 2 (centre detent, RGB LED, always active) and p.94 The Filter Knob: 'controls a low-pass filter when you turn it anticlockwise from the centre... high-pass filter when you turn it clockwise... detent in the centre – in this position, no filtering takes place and the LED below the knob is dimly lit white... The LED changes to pale blue when either filter type is active.'

p.16 item 5 labels the eight track buttons Synth 1 and 2 / MIDI 1 and 2 / Drum 1 to 4. Glossary 'Track' (p.14): 'When you press a Track button, you always go to Note View for that track.' (Item 5 does add 'operation varies slightly depending on other user actions,' but the glossary states the Note-View rule unconditionally.)

Second clause verbatim: p.16 item 6 'Note that Probability is the Shift function of the Pattern Settings button.' Nuance on first clause: the guide's own glossary/item-6 names the Step-button GROUP as 'Note, Velocity, Gate and Probability,' with Pattern Settings listed separately as item 7. Physically the four buttons in that row are labelled Note/Velocity/Gate/Pattern Settings (Probability being the Shift legend on Pattern Settings), so the claim is physically accurate but uses different naming than the manual's step-button group.

Glossary p.11 'Expanded View – Shift + Note: Doubles the area of performance pads for the Synths, and allows you create patterns on all drum tracks simultaneously, using the currently selected samples.' Drum tracks = all four.

p.11 glossary: 'Fixed – Shift + Velocity: Allows the velocity response of the grid pads to be disabled' (fixed value is 96, p.48). 'Micro step – Shift + Gate: The interval between consecutive Steps is further subdivided into six micro steps.'

All five confirmed: Mutate = Shift+Duplicate (p.11), Side Chain = Shift+FX (p.13/p.93), View Lock = Shift+Patterns (p.13/p.80), Packs = Shift+Projects ('Packs View is the secondary view of the Projects button. Open by holding Shift and pressing Projects', p.97), Setup Page = Shift+Save (p.13).
p.102: 'It is entered by holding down Shift and pressing Save, and exited by pressing Play.'

p.106 Advanced Setup View: 'entered by holding down Shift while powering the unit on, and exited by pressing Play.' Note toggles Easy Start/Mass Storage ('If Note is lit bright green, it is enabled, if Note is lit dim red, it is disabled'); Duplicate sets MIDI Thru ('When Duplicate is lit bright green, the MIDI Thru port will act as a second MIDI Out... dim red... regular MIDI Thru'); FX toggles Master Compressor ('enabled or disabled by pressing FX... bright green... dim red').

p.106 Save Lock: 'To enable Save Lock, hold both Shift and Save down while powering the unit on. While Save Lock is enabled, the Save button is unlit at all times. Save Lock status is retained during subsequent power cycles.'
p.107 Project loading problems: 'hold down both Shift and Clear while turning Circuit Tracks on' forces loading a blank Project — described as recovery for a corrupted/interrupted save.

p.108 Bootloader Mode: '1. Turn Circuit Tracks off 2. Hold down the Scales, Preset and Note buttons 3. Power Circuit Tracks on again... Bootloader Mode is most easily exited by simply pressing the Play button.'

p.18 rear item 4: 'MIDI In, Out and Thru – standard set of three MIDI connectors on 5-pin DIN sockets... the MIDI Thru port may be configured in Advanced Setup View to act as a clone of the MIDI Out port.' Full-size DIN, not TRS. Second-MIDI-Out reconfig confirmed p.106.
p.18 rear item 1: 'Outputs L/Mono and R... two ¼" TS jack sockets. Max. output level is +5.3 dBu (+/-1.5 dBu). With no plug in the R socket, the L/Mono socket carries a mono mix of L and R channels.'

p.18 rear item 5: 'Inputs 1 and 2 – two external audio inputs: line level signals... can be mixed with the internally-generated sounds, and can be individually treated by the FX section. They can also be ducked by the drum tracks. The inputs are unbalanced on ¼" TS jack sockets.'

p.18 item 2 + p.105 + p.87: 3.5 mm TRS Sync socket, 5 V amplitude, rate set in Setup View to 1/2/4/8/24 ppqn (pads 17-21), default 2 ppqn. 'Note that Swing (if set to something other than 50%) is not applied to the analogue clock output.'

p.18 rear item 3: 'connect a pair of stereo headphones here. The main outputs remain active when a plug is inserted. The headphone amplifier can drive +5 dBu into a pair of 150 ohm stereo headphones.' Note: the V3 PDF text does not itself state the '3.5 mm' connector size for the headphone jack (only the Sync jack is explicitly '3.5 mm TRS'); however 3.5 mm stereo is correct per Novation's published specs. Everything else verbatim.

p.18 rear item 6: 'USB-C port. This is also the DC power input... battery charging... The port is MIDI class compliant... Also used for firmware updates. NOTE – Circuit Tracks' USB port does not carry audio.'

p.9-10: 'The battery is non-removable and non-replaceable. Circuit Tracks will operate for up to 4 hours... When the battery capacity drops below 15%, a Low Battery symbol will be displayed... charging time is up to 4 hours.' All numeric facts confirmed. Caveat: the exact colour ('red') and 'grid symbol' descriptor come from the displayed icon image, not the prose (prose just says 'a Low Battery symbol'); the low-battery indicator is indeed shown red on the grid.

p.18 rear item 8: '"soft" on/off switch; to prevent inadvertent power up/down, a press of approx. one second is needed to turn the unit on or off. The button has an integral LED which lights green to indicate that the internal battery is charging.'

p.103 MIDI Channels table: Synth 1=1, Synth 2=2, MIDI 1=3, MIDI 2=4, Drums 1-4=10 (all four drums share ch.10). 'Each track... can be set to any of MIDI channels 1-15. Channel 16 is reserved for the Project.' 'No two tracks can transmit on the same MIDI channel.'

p.104 MIDI I/O table exactly: pads 25/26 Note (Green), 27/28 CC (Orange), 29/30 Program Change (Purple), 31/32 Clock (Pale Blue), each an Rx/Tx pair. 'By default, both MIDI Rx and MIDI Tx are ON (buttons brightly lit) for all data categories.'

p.103 Brightness: 'Pad 24 (illuminated white) controls the brightness of the grid pads. The default setting is for full brightness, but pressing Pad 24 dims them by approximately 50%... The brightness setting is saved when Circuit Tracks is powered down.'

p.85-86: internal 'range 40 to 240 BPM; the default tempo for a new Project is 120 BPM'; 'the internal tempo clock only allows integer BPMs'; external sync 'including fractional values - in the range 30 to 300 BPM.' Tap Tempo: 'Hold down Shift and tap the Tempo/Swing button... You need at least three taps... it will then calculate the BPM by averaging the last five taps.'

p.86: 'default value of 50 (the range is 20 to 80)'; diagrams show Swing=25% 'Even beats are advanced' and Swing=75% 'Even beats are delayed' (below 50 advances off-beats, above 50 delays them). 'Swing is adjusted with Macro 2, while in Tempo View.'

p.87 Click track: 'activated or deactivated by holding Shift and pressing Clear. Clear will light bright green when Click is enabled and dim red when it is not... To adjust the volume of the Click, press Tempo/Swing and use Macro 5.'

Glossary p.12: 'Playback Mode... the Play button will be lit bright green'; 'Record Mode... The Record button will be lit bright red'; 'Playback Cursor: the white pad which moves through the pattern display... Changes to red in Record Mode.'

p.17 item 15: 'In Note View they let you shift the pitch of the synth pads or the MIDI tracks up by one to five octaves, or down by one to six octaves... the pitch range of each track is adjustable independently.'

p.16 item 14: 'Each synth track can use any of 128 Patches, each MIDI track can use any of eight MIDI templates, and each drum track can use any of 64 percussion samples. The synth and drum Presets are arranged in pages of 32' (128=4 pages, 64=2 pages). Glossary Patch/Sample/Template corroborate.

p.16 item 9: 'Scales – opens Scales View: allows the selection of one of sixteen different music scales for the synth keyboard, and also lets you transpose the synth keyboard to a higher or lower key.' Glossary 'Scale View' confirms 16 scales.

p.90: 'Sixteen delay and eight reverb presets are provided... The send levels from each track... individually adjustable for each track using the Macro controls. There is also a master compressor which is applied to your mix by default.' Master compressor toggle via FX in Advanced Setup View confirmed p.106.

p.93: 'Seven Side Chain presets are available'; opened via 'holding Shift and pressing FX'; lets the selected drum track duck synth/external-input audio; 'the default condition is for Side Chain to be OFF on both synths and both MIDI tracks.' MIDI tracks represent the two external audio inputs.

p.88-89: eight-channel mixer, 'The illuminated pads on Row 1 are Mute buttons for each track.' Default macros = track volume (default 100, range 0-127). Pan: pressing the down-arrow enters pan — note the guide's arrow glyphs render as J/K; the default level button (J) is the DOWN arrow (▼) per Novation's own online guide, and pressing it 'converts the Macro controls to pan controls' (up-arrow K/▲ then lit; pressing it returns to level). Pan LED: white centre, increasingly bright blue left, bright pink right. All confirmed.

p.17 item 20: 'To enable Sticky Shift, enter Setup View and press Shift. Sticky Shift is on when the Shift button is bright green.' 'When you press Shift it acts as if it is held until you press it a second time.'

p.16 item 8: 'Step Page (1-16/17-32) – selects whether the Pattern for the currently selected track is initially 16 or 32 steps in length. When a 32-step Pattern is selected, the colour of the button legend changes when the sequence is running to indicate which "half" of the sequence the grid is currently displaying.'

Glossary: 'Pattern memory... there are eight per track in each Project'; 'Project... Up to 64 Projects can be saved'; 'Pack... Up to 32 Packs can be exported to a Micro SD card.' Minor internal wording nuance: the Packs section (p.97) phrases it as 'A card can hold 31 additional Packs,' but the glossary and spec list state 32 exportable Packs, matching the claim.

p.19: 'Long-press the POWER button, and the grid will show the boot-up display for approximately five seconds... After initial boot-up, the display will change colour from pale red to bright green sequentially from top left to bottom right, indicating Pack loading.'

p.7 What's In The Box: 'USB Type A to Type C cable (1.5 m)' and 'AC adaptor: 5 V DC, 2 A; includes interchangeable AC plugs.' p.9: adaptor has 'a Type A USB output... interchangeable slide-in AC plug heads.'
p.18 rear item 9: 'Kensington MiniSaver – secure your Circuit Tracks to a suitable structure if desired.'

Glossary 'Scene' (p.13): 'One of 16 memories to which multiple Patterns and Pattern Chains can be assigned... Button: Hold Shift (while Mixer View is selected).' p.81: 'Scenes are accessed in Mixer View... The two lower rows of pads in Mixer View represent the 16 Scenes.' Nuance: scenes are triggered by simply pressing the lower-row pads in Mixer View; holding Shift is specifically for ASSIGNING/storing patterns to a scene — but the glossary itself lists the button combo as 'Hold Shift while Mixer View selected,' matching the claim.

p.96: 'Save needs to be pressed twice to complete the store process: the first press will flash the Save button; a second press will save your work to the last Project memory that was in use... Note that you can "abort" the Save routine after the first press of Save by pressing any other button.'

p.96 Changing Project Colours: 'After pressing Save for the first time, the LED under Macro 1 rotary control will light in the current colour of the pad... You can now scroll through a palette of 14 colours by rotating the Macro 1 knob.'

verification.overallNotes:
DEVICE-CONFUSION AUDIT RESULT: No cross-device contamination found. All 43 claims are sourced from the official Circuit Tracks User Guide V3 (fael-downloads-prod.focusrite.com/.../circuit_tracks_user_guide_v3_en.pdf, © 2022 Focusrite), which is explicitly and exclusively about the Circuit Tracks. I extracted the full PDF text (pdftotext) and matched every claim to Tracks-specific pages — none belong to the original Circuit (2015) or Circuit Rhythm. Key Tracks-only markers confirming the right device: eight track buttons include MIDI 1/MIDI 2, full-size 5-pin DIN MIDI In/Out/Thru with Thru-as-second-Out, two external line inputs, USB-C, internal battery — all absent or different on the original Circuit. All 43 verdicts: CONFIRMED.

Two nuances worth flagging in the guide (not errors, but naming/wording subtleties):
1. hardware-5: The manual's official 'Step buttons' GROUP is named Note/Velocity/Gate/Probability (item 6 + glossary), while the physical fourth button is labelled 'Pattern Settings' (item 7) with Probability as its Shift function. The claim's mapping is physically accurate; just note the manual doesn't call Pattern Settings a 'step button.'
2. hardware-34: The guide renders the octave/page arrow buttons as glyphs that pdftotext extracts as 'J' and 'K'. Cross-referenced with Novation's online user guide: J = DOWN arrow (▼, lit by default for level), K = UP arrow (▲). So pan is indeed entered via the DOWN arrow — the claim is correct.

Important HARDWARE-angle facts the claim set MISSED entirely:
- Master Volume knob (rear/top item 4): a dedicated analogue-style overall output-level control, separate from the mixer track levels and the master filter. No claim covers it.
- Fixed Velocity's actual value is 96 (p.48) — the claims only say it 'disables velocity response.'
- Per-track identification colours (p.21): Synth 1 = Violet, Synth 2 = Pale Green, MIDI 1 = Blue, MIDI 2 = Pink, Drum 1 = Orange, Drum 2 = Yellow, Drum 3 = Purple, Drum 4 = Aqua — used consistently across views.
- Button interaction model (p.16): most buttons have a momentary (long-press = temporary view while held) vs latching (short-press = switch view) behaviour; Record is special (momentary press = punch-in/out).
- microSD requirements (p.100): Class 10 minimum, FAT32 format.
- Power sourcing detail (p.8-9): battery charges only from a USB 3.0 port when computer-powered; three power sources (USB 3.0 computer, supplied AC adaptor via USB-C, internal battery).
- Delay presets are BPM-synced (p.90-92) with a full preset table (ping-pong, swung, etc.); reverb presets range small chamber → hall.
- 16 demo Projects are pre-loaded from the factory (p.20-21).
- Firmware/patch/sample/template editing and backup are handled via Novation Components (Web-MIDI browser) over USB (p.101).
- The audio path is described as 'six internal tracks plus two external audio inputs' fed through the master filter (only the 2 synths + 4 drums generate audio; the 2 MIDI tracks do not).

verdicts.note: Top View item 1: '32-pad playing grid – a 4 x 8 matrix of pads; internally illuminated with RGB LEDs.' Intro: 'a set of 32 illuminated, velocity-sensitive pads.' Footnote: 'each pad has internal red, blue and green LEDs, each of which can light at different intensities.' All three sub-claims verbatim.

Item 3: 'Macro controls 1 to 8 – eight multi-functional rotary encoders, with associated RGB LED. The availability and function of these controls varies with Circuit Track's various views... The movement of Macro controls in performance may be recorded and replayed.' Verbatim.

Item 2: 'Master Filter – rotary control with a centre detent and RGB LED... It is always active.' Filter Knob (p94): 'low-pass filter when you turn it anticlockwise from the centre... high-pass filter when you turn it clockwise... the LED below the knob is dimly lit white... The LED changes to pale blue when either filter type is active.' Also called 'the large Master Filter knob.' All verbatim.
Item 5 lists the eight track buttons: 'Synth 1 and 2/MIDI 1 and 2/Drum 1 to 4.' Glossary Track entry: 'When you press a Track button, you always go to Note View for that track.' Confirmed.

Item 6: 'Step buttons: Note, Velocity, Gate and Probability... Note that Probability is the Shift function of the Pattern Settings button.' Item 7 lists Pattern Settings as a separate physical button. So the physical fourth button is Pattern Settings and Probability = Shift+Pattern Settings, exactly as claimed. Minor terminology note: the glossary's 'Step buttons' entry names the group 'Note, Velocity, Gate and Probability' (labeling by function rather than by physical button), but this does not contradict the claim.

Glossary: 'Expanded View | Shift + Note | Doubles the area of performance pads for the Synths, and allows you create patterns on all drum tracks simultaneously, using the currently selected samples.' 'all drum tracks' = all four. Verbatim.

Glossary: 'Fixed | Shift + Velocity | Allows the velocity response of the grid pads to be disabled.' 'Micro step | Shift + Gate | The interval between consecutive Steps is further subdivided into six micro steps.' Verbatim.

Verified each: Mutate = Shift+Duplicate (glossary); Side Chain = Shift+FX (p93); View Lock = Shift+Patterns (glossary/p80); Packs = 'secondary view of the Projects button... holding Shift and pressing Projects' (p97); Setup Page = Shift+Save (glossary/p102). All five confirmed.
p102: 'It is entered by holding down Shift and pressing Save, and exited by pressing Play.' Verbatim.

p106: 'entered by holding down Shift while powering the unit on, and exited by pressing Play.' Note toggles Easy Start/Mass Storage ('press the Note button. If Note is lit bright green, it is enabled, if Note is lit dim red, it is disabled'); Duplicate toggles MIDI Thru ('When Duplicate is lit bright green, the MIDI Thru port will act as a second MIDI Out'); FX toggles Master Compressor ('enabled or disabled by pressing FX while in Advanced Setup View'). All verbatim.

p107: 'To enable Save Lock, hold both Shift and Save down while powering the unit on. While Save Lock is enabled, the Save button is unlit at all times. Save Lock status is retained during subsequent power cycles.' Verbatim.

p107 (Project loading problems): 'we have included a method of turning Circuit Tracks on and forcing it to load a blank Project instead. To do this, hold down both Shift and Clear while turning Circuit Tracks on.' Verbatim.

p108: '1. Turn Circuit Tracks off 2. Hold down the Scales, Preset and Note buttons 3. Power Circuit Tracks on again.' 'Bootloader Mode is most easily exited by simply pressing the Play button.' Verbatim.

Rear item 4: 'MIDI In, Out and Thru – standard set of three MIDI connectors on 5-pin DIN sockets... the MIDI Thru port may be configured in Advanced Setup View to act as a clone of the MIDI Out port.' Key Features: 'Full size 5-pin MIDI In, Out and Thru.' Confirms full-size DIN (not TRS) and Thru-as-second-Out. Verbatim.

Rear item 1: 'Outputs - L/Mono and R... on two 1/4" TS jack sockets. Max. output level is +5.3 dBu (+/-1.5 dBu). With no plug in the R socket, the L/Mono socket carries a mono mix of L and R channels.' Verbatim.

Rear item 5: 'Inputs 1 and 2 – two external audio inputs: line level signals connected here can be mixed with the internally-generated sounds, and can be individually treated by the FX section. They can also be ducked by the drum tracks. The inputs are unbalanced on 1/4" TS jack sockets.' Verbatim.

Rear item 2: 'Sync – a 3.5 mm TRS jack socket supplying a clock signal of 5 V amplitude... The default rate is two pulses per quarter note.' p105 Analogue clock rates: 'select the rate to be 1, 2, 4, 8 or 24 ppqn... The default value is 2 ppqn' and 'Swing (if set to something other than 50%) is not applied to the analogue clock output.' All verbatim.

Rear item 3: '(Headphones) – connect a pair of stereo headphones here. The main outputs remain active when a plug is inserted. The headphone amplifier can drive +5 dBu into a pair of 150 ohm stereo headphones.' The load-bearing specs (mains stay active, +5 dBu into 150 ohm) are verbatim. The '3.5 mm' connector size is not stated in the guide text (the rear diagram uses a symbol), but it is factually correct for the hardware — flagging only that the size is not textually sourced from this guide.

Rear item 6: 'USB-C port. This is also the DC power input to the unit for external powering and battery charging... The port is MIDI class compliant... NOTE – Circuit Tracks' USB port does not carry audio.' Verbatim.

p10: 'The battery is non-removable and non-replaceable. Circuit Tracks will operate for up to 4 hours... When the battery capacity drops below 15%, a Low Battery symbol will be displayed... charging time is up to 4 hours.' All verbatim. The symbol's colour ('red') is shown as an image, not stated as text, but is correct for the device.

Rear item 8: "'soft' on/off switch; to prevent inadvertent power up/down, a press of approx. one second is needed to turn the unit on or off. The button has an integral LED which lights green to indicate that the internal battery is charging." Verbatim.

p103 table: Synth1=1, Synth2=2, MIDI1=3, MIDI2=4, Drums1-4=10. 'can be set to any of MIDI channels 1-15. Channel 16 is reserved for the Project... No two tracks can transmit on the same MIDI channel.' All verbatim.

p104 table: pads 25/26 Note Rx/Tx (Green), 27/28 CC Rx/Tx (Orange), 29/30 Program Change Rx/Tx (Purple), 31/32 Clock Rx/Tx (Pale Blue). 'By default, both MIDI Rx and MIDI Tx are ON.' Pair order (Rx then Tx) and colours all match. Verbatim.

p103: 'Pad 24 (illuminated white) controls the brightness of the grid pads. The default setting is for full brightness, but pressing Pad 24 dims them by approximately 50%... The brightness setting is saved when Circuit Tracks is powered down.' Verbatim.

p85: 'range 40 to 240 BPM; the default tempo for a new Project is 120 BPM'; 'the internal tempo clock only allows integer BPMs'; 'will synchronise to any external clock rates – including fractional values - in the range 30 to 300 BPM.' p86 Tap Tempo: 'Hold down Shift and tap the Tempo/Swing button... You need at least three taps... calculate the BPM by averaging the last five taps.' All verbatim.

p86: 'Altering the Swing parameter from its default value of 50 (the range is 20 to 80) alters the timing of even-numbered steps (the off-beats); a lower swing value shortens the time between an even step and the previous odd step [= advances the even step], a higher Swing value has the opposite effect [= delays it].' 'Swing is adjusted with Macro 2, while in Tempo View.' The prose supports the claim's advance/delay direction; the diagram captions are garbled by PDF extraction but the prose is definitive. Confirmed.

p87: 'The Click (or metronome) can be activated or deactivated by holding Shift and pressing Clear. Clear will light bright green when Click is enabled and dim red when it is not... To adjust the volume of the Click, press Tempo/Swing and use Macro 5.' Verbatim.

Glossary: 'Playback Mode... the Play button will be lit bright green.' 'Record Mode... The Record button will be lit bright red.' 'Playback Cursor... the white pad which moves through the pattern display... Changes to red in Record Mode.' All verbatim.

Item 15: 'In Note View they let you shift the pitch of the synth pads or the MIDI tracks up by one to five octaves, or down by one to six octaves when entering notes: the pitch range of each track is adjustable independently.' Verbatim (5 up / 6 down).

Item 14: 'Each synth track can use any of 128 Patches, each MIDI track can use any of eight MIDI templates, and each drum track can use any of 64 percussion samples. The synth and drum Presets are arranged in pages of 32.' Glossary Patch (128, four pages of 32), Sample (64, two pages of 32), Template (8) confirm the page breakdown. Verbatim.

Item 9: 'Scales – opens Scales View: allows the selection of one of sixteen different music scales for the synth keyboard, and also lets you transpose the synth keyboard to a higher or lower key.' p30 confirms 16 scales (only Chromatic exceeds 8 notes, with 12). Verbatim.

p90: 'Sixteen delay and eight reverb presets are provided... send levels from each track... individually adjustable for each track using the Macro controls.' Master compressor 'applied to your mix by default' and 'enabled or disabled by pressing FX while in Advanced Setup View' (p106). Note: p92 cross-reference erroneously says 'Setup View', but the authoritative Advanced Setup View section (p106) confirms it is Advanced Setup View, as the claim states. Confirmed.

p93: 'Seven Side Chain presets are available, each allowing the selected drum track to modify the sound of the synth tracks (or signals at the external audio inputs)... The default state is for Side Chain to be OFF on both synths and both MIDI tracks. Side Chain View is the secondary view of the FX button. Open by holding Shift and pressing FX.' Verbatim.

p88: 'eight-channel mixer... all the tracks play at a volume level of 100 (arbitrary units, range 0-127)... The illuminated pads on Row 1 are Mute buttons for each track... By default... the Macros control volume level... Pressing the [arrow] button converts the Macro controls to pan controls... default pan position... Macro LEDs showing white. Panning a track left turns the LED increasingly bright blue; panning to the right turns it increasingly bright pink.' Levels/mutes/pan colours all verbatim. Caveat: the guide's arrow-button glyphs are rendered ambiguously in the extracted text (both arrows appear as similar symbols); the claim's identification of the pan-toggle as the 'down-arrow' matches the device's real-world behavior but the specific arrow direction is not unambiguously legible in the source text.

Item 20: 'To avoid holding the Shift button you can enable Sticky Shift. When you press Shift it acts as if it is held until you press it a second time. To enable Sticky Shift, enter Setup View and press Shift. Sticky Shift is on when the Shift button is bright green.' Verbatim.

Item 8: 'Step Page (1-16/17-32) – selects whether the Pattern for the currently selected track is initially 16 or 32 steps in length. When a 32-step Pattern is selected, the colour of the button legend changes when the sequence is running to indicate which half of the sequence the grid is currently displaying.' Verbatim (p75 confirms: dim blue <=16, bright blue Page1, orange Page2).

Glossary Pattern memory: 'eight per track in each Project.' Project: 'Up to 64 Projects.' Glossary Pack: 'Up to 32 Packs can be exported to a Micro SD card' — matches the claim verbatim. Nuance worth flagging for the guide: the detailed microSD section (p97/p99) states the card holds '31 additional Packs', for 'up to 32 Packs' available total including the internal one. So the '32' figure is the total accessible, of which 31 physically reside on the card.

p19: 'the grid will show the boot-up display for approximately five seconds... After initial boot-up, the display will change colour from pale red to bright green sequentially from top left to bottom right, indicating Pack loading.' Verbatim.

p7 What's In The Box: 'USB Type A to Type C cable (1.5 m)' and 'AC adaptor: 5 V DC, 2 A; includes interchangeable AC plugs.' p9: '5 V DC, 2 A type with a Type A USB output... interchangeable slide-in AC plug heads.' All verbatim.
Rear item 9: 'Kensington MiniSaver – secure your Circuit Tracks to a suitable structure if desired.' Verbatim.

Glossary Scene: 'One of 16 memories to which multiple Patterns and Pattern Chains can be assigned... [Hold Shift (while Mixer View is selected)].' p81: 'Scenes are accessed in Mixer View... The two lower rows of pads in Mixer View represent the 16 Scenes.' Assignment uses Shift + Scene pad in Mixer View. Confirmed (the claim's 'medium' confidence is well supported by the source).

p96: 'Save needs to be pressed twice to complete the store process: the first press will flash the Save button; a second press will save your work to the last Project memory that was in use... Note that you can abort the Save routine after the first press of Save by pressing any other button.' Verbatim.

p96 Changing Project Colours: 'After pressing Save for the first time, the LED under Macro 1 rotary control will light in the current colour... You can now scroll through a palette of 14 colours by rotating the Macro 1 knob.' Verbatim.

verification.overallNotes:
All 43 hardware claims were verified against the primary source cited by every claim: the official Novation Circuit Tracks User Guide, Version 3 (109 pages, (c) 2022 Focusrite Audio Engineering), fetched from Novation's own CDN (fael-downloads-prod.focusrite.com/customer/prod/downloads/circuit_tracks_user_guide_v3_en.pdf) and read in full. This is Circuit-Tracks-specific material (title page, Key Features, Top/Rear View, and all body sections reference the two synths + two MIDI + four drum architecture and the microSD/Packs/battery feature set unique to Tracks), so there is no cross-contamination from the original 2015 Circuit or the Circuit Rhythm. Every claim is CONFIRMED; page-number citations in the claims all match the guide.

Minor accuracy caveats surfaced (none change a verdict):
- hardware-32 / hardware-10 (Master Compressor location): The guide is internally inconsistent. Page 92 cross-references the Master Compressor toggle to 'Setup View', but the authoritative Advanced Setup View section (p106) states it is toggled by FX in ADVANCED Setup View. The claims correctly use Advanced Setup View. Flag this p92 typo if quoting the guide.
- hardware-37 (Packs): The glossary says 'up to 32 Packs can be exported to a Micro SD card' (matching the claim), but the detailed microSD section (p97/p99) clarifies the card holds 31 additional Packs, giving 32 total accessible including the internal Pack. If the guide states '32 on the card' verbatim it is slightly imprecise about where the 32nd lives.
- hardware-18 (headphones) and hardware-20 (low-battery symbol): The '3.5 mm' headphone connector size and the 'red' colour of the low-battery symbol are correct for the device but are conveyed by diagrams/images in the guide rather than by explicit body text; the load-bearing electrical specs (+5 dBu/150 ohm, <15% threshold, non-removable battery, 4h run / 4h charge) are stated verbatim.
- hardware-34 (Mixer pan toggle): pdftotext mangles the up/down arrow glyphs (both surface as look-alike letters), so the specific 'down-arrow' identity is not unambiguously legible from the source text, though the pan function and LED colours are verbatim and the down-arrow=pan mapping matches real-world device behavior.
- hardware-5: The claim names the four physical step buttons as 'Note, Velocity, Gate and Pattern Settings' (correct physically), whereas the guide's Glossary 'Step buttons' entry names the group 'Note, Velocity, Gate and Probability' (labeled by function). Not a contradiction — Probability is Shift+Pattern Settings, exactly as the claim states.

Notable hardware-angle facts the claim set MISSED entirely:
1. Master Volume knob (Top View item 4) — a dedicated analogue-style rotary controlling overall output level of ALL audio outputs; distinct from the per-track Mixer levels. No claim covers it.
2. Per-synth polyphony is 6 voices (Key Features: 'Two synth tracks with 6-voice polyphony'; p35 'six-note polyphonic') — a core spec, only tangentially implied.
3. Track colour-coding (used throughout the hardware/LED behavior): Synth 1 violet, Synth 2 pale green, MIDI 1 blue, MIDI 2 pink, Drum 1 orange, Drum 2 yellow, Drum 3 purple, Drum 4 aqua (p21).
4. microSD card requirements: Class 10 minimum, FAT32 format (p100) — a hardware compatibility constraint.
5. Power: three supply methods (computer USB, AC adaptor, internal battery); charging specifically requires a USB 3.0 port; adaptor accepts 100-240 V, 50/60 Hz (p9-10).
6. Drum tracks use only the even-numbered Macros (2/4/6/8 = Pitch, Decay, Distortion, EQ); odd Macros are inactive on drums (p63). Relevant to Macro/hardware behavior.
7. Guide is firmware V3 / (c) 2022 — feature set (e.g., MIDI Thru-as-Out, Save Lock) is firmware-dependent; earlier firmware may differ.
